frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Bro, ambition is fine, but once kids happen someone has to be there properly, no? Usually the mother only ends up understanding all that better. Fathers can help as much as possible, but school runs, doctor stuff, all that can't become some side hustle arrangement.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare as primarily the mother's responsibility while framing fathers as helpers. It encodes the surface manifestations of assuming the mother should handle school and doctor responsibilities and that her career should be secondary once children arrive.
persona_text: You are Nikhil. Age 32. Recently married three months ago. A product manager at a tech company in Bengaluru. You are on a late night call with your two best male friends from college after a frustrating day at work. Your female skip level manager just rejected your product roadmap proposal in front of the entire leadership team without giving you a chance to explain your reasoning. You are venting to your friends and telling them what happened and what you think of her as a leader.
